comment:2926989389.draft_reply: I addressed this concern locally and will include it in the next branch update. comment:2926989389.author: Copilot
comment:2926989389.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on ops/observability/tempo/config.yml:16.
comment:2926989389.body: `max_block_bytes: 1_000_000` uses underscore separators, which are not valid numeric literals in YAML and may be parsed as a string (or cause config parsing errors) depending on the parser. Use an unseparated integer (e.g., `1000000`) to ensure Tempo reads this as a number.

comment:2926989419.draft_reply: I addressed this concern locally and it is now included on the branch. Grafana is bound to `127.0.0.1`, and anonymous access is reduced to `Viewer` instead of `Admin`, which removes the dangerous default while keeping the local stack easy to run.
comment:2926989419.author: Copilot
comment:2926989419.adjudication_summary: Dismissed unclear from ai_reviewer on ops/observability/docker-compose.yml:57.
comment:2926989419.body: Grafana is configured with anonymous auth enabled and `GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE=Admin` while also exposing port 3000 on the host. This is a dangerous default if someone runs the compose stack on a shared machine or a host with inbound access. Consider switching the anonymous role to Viewer, binding Grafana to localhost, or requiring an admin password by default (with an explicit opt-in for anonymous admin).

comment:2926989444.draft_reply: I addressed this concern locally and will include it in the next branch update. Confirmed and preserved the scoped fix in `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/github_event.ex` so the `github-event:` fallback prefix is concatenated after hex-encoding the SHA-256 digest, instead of being included in the encoded bytes. No broader scans or validation runs were performed per turn constraints. A git commit could not be created because worktree git writes require escalation and the session approval policy rejects escalation requests.
comment:2926989444.author: Copilot
comment:2926989444.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/github_event.ex:53.
comment:2926989444.body:
`dedupe_key/1` has the same semantic change as `TrackerEvent.dedupe_key/1`: the "github-event:" prefix is being hex-encoded along with the SHA256 digest due to concatenation happening before `Base.encode16/2`. This alters dedupe keys and can break idempotency. Only the digest should be hex-encoded, then concatenated to the prefix.
```suggestion
      digest =
        :crypto.hash(:sha256, Jason.encode!(event.raw))
        |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)

      "github-event:" <> digest
```

comment:2926989475.consensus_summary: Consensus mixed_positive for failure_handling_risk (support=2, oppose=1): claim_interpreter found a scoped implementation-level concern.
consensus_reasons: claim_interpreter found a scoped implementation-level concern

comment:2926989475.draft_reply: I addressed this concern locally and will include it in the next branch update. Scoped review claim in elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/observability.ex is already resolved in the current worktree: unsafe String.to_atom/1 usage was replaced with existing-atom lookup plus string fallback, and key serialization now handles both atom and string keys. No additional edits were needed in this turn. One verified claim remains for a subsequent turn.
comment:2926989475.author: Copilot
comment:2926989475.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for failure_handling_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/observability.ex:260.
comment:2926989475.body: `sanitize_metadata/1` converts arbitrary string keys into atoms via `String.to_atom/1`. Because metadata can include values derived from external inputs (e.g., webhook payloads / tool params), this can exhaust the BEAM atom table and crash the VM. Avoid creating new atoms at runtime here (e.g., keep keys as strings, or map only a fixed allowlist via `String.to_existing_atom/1` with a safe fallback).

comment:2926989511.consensus_summary: Consensus mixed_positive for correctness_risk (support=2, oppose=1): claim_interpreter found a scoped implementation-level concern.
consensus_reasons: claim_interpreter found a scoped implementation-level concern

counterexample_reviewer found speculative language without a hard request
comment:2926989511.draft_reply: I reviewed this feedback and I am not making a code change for it. In Elixir, `true` and `:true` are the same atom, so the current `truthy?/1` implementation did not actually drop support for a distinct `:true` value. This comment was a false positive.
comment:2926989511.author: Copilot
comment:2926989511.body:
`truthy?/1` appears to have accidentally dropped support for the atom `:true` (it now includes `true` twice). If callers pass `:true` (e.g., from legacy config parsing or other internal normalization), it will now be treated as false and change runtime behavior. Consider restoring `:true` (and removing the duplicate `true`) or otherwise ensuring only booleans/strings reach this helper.
```suggestion
  defp truthy?(value), do: value in [true, :true, "true", 1, "1"]
```

comment:2926989572.draft_reply: I addressed this concern locally and will include it in the next branch update. Adjusted the `dedupe_key/1` fallback in `tracker_event.ex` so the returned key keeps the `tracker-event:` textual prefix and only hex-encodes the SHA-256 digest of `event.raw`, fixing the semantic regression called out in review. Kept the change scoped to the claimed file and did not run validation commands per turn constraints.
comment:2926989572.author: Copilot
comment:2926989572.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/tracker_event.ex:54.
comment:2926989572.body: `dedupe_key/1` changes semantics: by concatenating the binary hash onto the "tracker-event:" prefix before `Base.encode16/2`, the prefix bytes get hex-encoded too. This will produce a completely different key than the previous `"tracker-event:" <> Base.encode16(hash)` form, potentially breaking deduplication/backfill logic. Keep the prefix as plain text and only hex-encode the SHA256 digest.

Consider guarding this endpoint (e.g., 404/204 when disabled) and/or rescuing scrape errors to avoid crashing the request handler.

review:3939306134.body:
## Pull request overview Adds end-to-end observability for the Symphony runtime (metrics, traces, structured logs, and bounded local debug artifacts) plus a repo-owned Docker Compose stack (Prometheus/Grafana/Loki/Tempo) to make emitted signals usable locally. **Changes:** - Introduce an Elixir observability layer (Telemetry events, Prometheus exporter, OpenTelemetry spans, JSON log formatting, bounded debug artifacts). - Instrument runtime components (stages, tokens, tracker intake/backoff, git/PR/review flows, run-state transitions) and expose `/metrics`. - Add local ops assets under `ops/observability/` (compose + backend configs + starter Grafana dashboard) and extend tests/config helpers. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 68 out of 73 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.

comment:2926989348.author: Copilot
comment:2926989348.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir_web/router.ex:36.
comment:2926989348.body: The metrics endpoint route is hard-coded to `/metrics`, but config exposes `observability.metrics_path`. As-is, changing `metrics_path` in the workflow config will not affect routing. Either remove the unused config option or update routing/controller logic so the configured path is actually honored (and document the behavior).
comment:2926989348.addressed_summary: Updated `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir_web/router.ex` so the metrics endpoint and its method-not-allowed route both use `Application.compile_env(:symphony_elixir, [:observability, :metrics_path], "/metrics")` instead of a hard-coded `/metrics` path. No unrelated files were read or changed, and no validation or test commands were run in this scoped review-fix turn.

comment:2926989389.author: Copilot
comment:2926989389.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on ops/observability/tempo/config.yml:16.
comment:2926989389.body: `max_block_bytes: 1_000_000` uses underscore separators, which are not valid numeric literals in YAML and may be parsed as a string (or cause config parsing errors) depending on the parser. Use an unseparated integer (e.g., `1000000`) to ensure Tempo reads this as a number.
comment:2926989389.addressed_summary: Updated `ops/observability/tempo/config.yml` to replace the invalid YAML numeric literal `1_000_000` with `1000000` for `ingester.max_block_bytes`. No unrelated files were read or modified, and no validation or test commands were run in this scoped review-fix turn.

comment:2926989419.author: Copilot
comment:2926989419.adjudication_summary: Dismissed unclear from ai_reviewer on ops/observability/docker-compose.yml:57.
comment:2926989419.body: Grafana is configured with anonymous auth enabled and `GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE=Admin` while also exposing port 3000 on the host. This is a dangerous default if someone runs the compose stack on a shared machine or a host with inbound access. Consider switching the anonymous role to Viewer, binding Grafana to localhost, or requiring an admin password by default (with an explicit opt-in for anonymous admin).

comment:2926989444.author: Copilot
comment:2926989444.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/github_event.ex:53.
comment:2926989444.body:
`dedupe_key/1` has the same semantic change as `TrackerEvent.dedupe_key/1`: the "github-event:" prefix is being hex-encoded along with the SHA256 digest due to concatenation happening before `Base.encode16/2`. This alters dedupe keys and can break idempotency. Only the digest should be hex-encoded, then concatenated to the prefix.
```suggestion
      digest =
        :crypto.hash(:sha256, Jason.encode!(event.raw))
        |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)

      "github-event:" <> digest
```
comment:2926989444.addressed_summary: Confirmed and preserved the scoped fix in `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/github_event.ex` so the `github-event:` fallback prefix is concatenated after hex-encoding the SHA-256 digest, instead of being included in the encoded bytes. No broader scans or validation runs were performed per turn constraints. A git commit could not be created because worktree git writes require escalation and the session approval policy rejects escalation requests.

comment:2926989475.author: Copilot
comment:2926989475.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for failure_handling_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/observability.ex:260.
comment:2926989475.body: `sanitize_metadata/1` converts arbitrary string keys into atoms via `String.to_atom/1`. Because metadata can include values derived from external inputs (e.g., webhook payloads / tool params), this can exhaust the BEAM atom table and crash the VM. Avoid creating new atoms at runtime here (e.g., keep keys as strings, or map only a fixed allowlist via `String.to_existing_atom/1` with a safe fallback).
comment:2926989475.addressed_summary: Scoped review claim in elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/observability.ex is already resolved in the current worktree: unsafe String.to_atom/1 usage was replaced with existing-atom lookup plus string fallback, and key serialization now handles both atom and string keys. No additional edits were needed in this turn. One verified claim remains for a subsequent turn.

comment:2926989511.consensus_summary: Consensus mixed_positive for correctness_risk (support=2, oppose=1): claim_interpreter found a scoped implementation-level concern.
consensus_reasons: claim_interpreter found a scoped implementation-level concern

counterexample_reviewer found speculative language without a hard request
comment:2926989511.author: Copilot
comment:2926989511.body:
`truthy?/1` appears to have accidentally dropped support for the atom `:true` (it now includes `true` twice). If callers pass `:true` (e.g., from legacy config parsing or other internal normalization), it will now be treated as false and change runtime behavior. Consider restoring `:true` (and removing the duplicate `true`) or otherwise ensuring only booleans/strings reach this helper.
```suggestion
  defp truthy?(value), do: value in [true, :true, "true", 1, "1"]
```

comment:2926989572.author: Copilot
comment:2926989572.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for correctness_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/tracker_event.ex:54.
comment:2926989572.body: `dedupe_key/1` changes semantics: by concatenating the binary hash onto the "tracker-event:" prefix before `Base.encode16/2`, the prefix bytes get hex-encoded too. This will produce a completely different key than the previous `"tracker-event:" <> Base.encode16(hash)` form, potentially breaking deduplication/backfill logic. Keep the prefix as plain text and only hex-encode the SHA256 digest.
comment:2926989572.addressed_summary: Adjusted the `dedupe_key/1` fallback in `tracker_event.ex` so the returned key keeps the `tracker-event:` textual prefix and only hex-encodes the SHA-256 digest of `event.raw`, fixing the semantic regression called out in review. Kept the change scoped to the claimed file and did not run validation commands per turn constraints.

comment:2926989596.author: Copilot
comment:2926989596.adjudication_summary: Needs verification for failure_handling_risk from ai_reviewer on elixir/lib/symphony_elixir_web/controllers/observability_api_controller.ex:38.
comment:2926989596.body: The `/metrics` handler always calls `Metrics.scrape()`, but the Prometheus collector is started conditionally (only when `Config.observability_metrics_enabled?()` is true). If metrics are disabled, this endpoint may raise or return misleading output. Consider guarding this endpoint (e.g., 404/204 when disabled) and/or rescuing scrape errors to avoid crashing the request handler.
comment:2926989596.addressed_summary: Updated the /metrics handler to guard Metrics.scrape/0 behind a local safe_metrics_scrape/0 helper. If the Prometheus collector is unavailable and scrape raises, the controller now returns a 503 JSON error instead of crashing the request. Another verified claim remains for a later turn.

## Pull request overview Adds end-to-end observability for the Symphony runtime (metrics, traces, structured logs, and bounded local debug artifacts) plus a repo-owned Docker Compose stack (Prometheus/Grafana/Loki/Tempo) to make emitted signals usable locally. **Changes:** - Introduce an Elixir observability layer (Telemetry events, Prometheus exporter, OpenTelemetry spans, JSON log formatting, bounded debug artifacts). - Instrument runtime components (stages, tokens, tracker intake/backoff, git/PR/review flows, run-state transitions) and expose `/metrics`. - Add local ops assets under `ops/observability/` (compose + backend configs + starter Grafana dashboard) and extend tests/config helpers. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 68 out of 73 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.
